introSection: This article is for a steel kiddie roller coaster currently under construction at Palace Playland in Old Orchard Beach, Maine, USA.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The coaster was originally located at Edaville Family Theme Park in Carver, Massachusetts, USA. It opened as part of the Thomas Land themed area on August 15, 2015, under the name of Troublesome Trucks Runaway Coaster. In 2022, following a period of closure, the coaster was renamed as Matterhorn Mine Co. once the Thomas & Friends license was removed from the area.

After Edaville closed as an amusement park in 2024, the coaster was one of the few listed rides that were not put up for sale in the auction. In May 2025, it was reported that the coaster was being dismantled during the auction and had been sold separately, which was soon revealed to be Palace Playland after parts had shown up in the park's storage area. During the park's post-season, the coaster was constructed on the former site of Orient Express.

sections.title: Design
sections.content:
The coaster was initially themed around the Troublesome Trucks from the Thomas & Friends cartoon series. It was one of three Thomas & Friends themed coasters at the time, the other two being Troublesome Trucks Runaway Coaster at Drayton Manor and Rock 'n' Roll Duncan (now Nia and Animal Coaster) at Fuji-Q Highland.

As Matterhorn Mine Co., the cars were themed after mine carts.

fullText:
This article is for a steel kiddie roller coaster currently under construction at Palace Playland in Old Orchard Beach, Maine, USA.

The coaster was originally located at Edaville Family Theme Park in Carver, Massachusetts, USA. It opened as part of the Thomas Land themed area on August 15, 2015, under the name of Troublesome Trucks Runaway Coaster. In 2022, following a period of closure, the coaster was renamed as Matterhorn Mine Co. once the Thomas & Friends license was removed from the area.

After Edaville closed as an amusement park in 2024, the coaster was one of the few listed rides that were not put up for sale in the auction. In May 2025, it was reported that the coaster was being dismantled during the auction and had been sold separately, which was soon revealed to be Palace Playland after parts had shown up in the park's storage area. During the park's post-season, the coaster was constructed on the former site of Orient Express.

The coaster was initially themed around the Troublesome Trucks from the Thomas & Friends cartoon series. It was one of three Thomas & Friends themed coasters at the time, the other two being Troublesome Trucks Runaway Coaster at Drayton Manor and Rock 'n' Roll Duncan (now Nia and Animal Coaster) at Fuji-Q Highland.

As Matterhorn Mine Co., the cars were themed after mine carts.
